<!-- Generated by spec-lite | agent: plan_feature | date: 2026-05-29 -->

# Feature: Flow — Readable Aliases, Copyable from UI, and Full JSONPath References

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-001
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Make flow request-chaining reliable and discoverable for end users by solving three
linked problems:

1. **Full JSONPath in flow variable references.** `flowResolver` currently uses a custom,
   limited dot-path traversal. Bring it up to the full JSONPath spec (recursive descent,
   wildcards, filter expressions, slices) using `jsonpath-plus` — the same engine
   `validationEngine` already uses — so references behave consistently across the product.
2. **Readable aliases.** Generate human-friendly aliases (e.g. `get-employee`) instead of
   opaque ones, so a reference like `{{get-employee.$body:$.data.id}}` reads clearly.
3. **Discoverable aliases.** Users currently have no easy way to see or copy a node's alias.
   Add a copy affordance on the flow node's hover info so users never have to guess.

**End-user outcome:** A user wiring one request's response into the next can hover a node,
copy its readable alias, and write a reference using any valid JSONPath expression — and it
resolves the same way the validation engine would resolve it.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TS strict, React 19, Vitest + RTL, adapter pattern, no platform APIs in
> `core`, JSDoc on public APIs) live in `.spec-lite/memory.md` and are **not** repeated here.
> Only additions/overrides for this feature are listed.

### Approach

**Part A — JSONPath engine (shared util in `core`).**
Add `jsonpath-plus` as a dependency of `packages/core` (it is pure JS — no Node/browser
APIs — so it does not violate the platform-agnostic constraint; `ajv` is already a core dep
as precedent). Create a single canonical helper `packages/core/src/utils/jsonPath.ts`
that mirrors `validationEngine.evaluateJsonPath`:
- Parse the body string as JSON.
- Run `JSONPath({ path, json, eval: false })` — **`eval: false` is mandatory** to block
  script injection via crafted filter expressions (parity with `validationEngine`).
- Return `{ value, values, found, error? }` where `value` is the first match (for scalar
  substitution) and `values` is the full result array.
`validationEngine` (in `packages/shared`) is **left unchanged** to avoid cross-package
dependency churn; a TODO is filed to consolidate the two later.

**Part B — New variable-reference grammar in `flowResolver`.**
Replace the custom `parseFlowPath` / `traverseJsonPath` / body branch of `resolveBodyPath`
with a new parser + JSONPath-backed body resolution. The new grammar uses an **explicit
`$`-rooted JSONPath after the section marker**, separated by a colon (user-selected design):

```
{{ [alias.] $section [ :subpath ] }}

$section := $body | $headers | $status | $statusText

$body    :subpath -> a JSONPath expression. Canonical form is $-rooted (e.g. :$.data.id).
                     For convenience the parser prepends "$." when the subpath does not
                     already start with "$" (so :data.id is treated as $.data.id).
                     Omitted -> whole body.
$headers :subpath -> a header name (case-insensitive). Omitted -> all headers as JSON.
$status            -> no subpath (the numeric status code).
$statusText        -> no subpath.
```

Examples:

```
{{ getEmployee.$body:$.data.id }}                 explicit alias + JSONPath
{{ getEmployee.$body:$.users[?(@.active)].id }}   filter expression (first match)
{{ getEmployee.$body:$..id }}                      recursive descent
{{ getEmployee.$body:$.items[0:2] }}               array slice
{{ getEmployee.$headers:content-type }}            header by name
{{ getEmployee.$status }}                           status code
{{ $body:$.data.id }}                               no alias -> search all allowed aliases
{{ $.data.id }}                                      shorthand -> implies $body, no alias
```

Parsing rule: locate the first section keyword (`$body`/`$headers`/`$status`/`$statusText`).
Text before it (minus a trailing `.`) is the alias (empty = none). Text after `:` is the
subpath. If **no** section keyword is present but the reference starts with `$` or a bare
key (e.g. `$.data.id`), treat it as a `$body` shorthand with no alias (searches all allowed
aliases, most-recent-first — preserving today's shorthand behavior).

Aliases are matched literally (no `$`, `.`, or `:` allowed inside an alias — guaranteed by
the alias generator in Part C). `flowContextToDynamicEnvVars` keys env vars by the exact
inner placeholder text, so colons / `$` / `[` / `?` / `@` in references are safe for the
downstream exact-match substitution.

**Part C — Readable alias generation in `requestNodeBuilder`.**
Rework `buildAliasBase`:
- Lowercase.
- Replace every run of non-alphanumeric characters with a single `-`; trim leading/trailing `-`.
- Cap length at `MAX_ALIAS_BASE_LENGTH` (keep 20).
- If the cleaned base has no alphanumeric content, fall back to `alias` (was `request`).
- Uniqueness: append `-N` ordinals starting at **2** on in-flow collision
  (`get-employee`, `get-employee-2`, `get-employee-3`). Comparison stays case-insensitive.

Cross-flow consistency is **best-effort by construction**: because the base derives
deterministically from the request name, the same request added as the sole instance gets
the same alias in every flow. Strict global determinism is explicitly *not* pursued; the
copy UI (Part D) closes the discoverability gap instead.

**Part D — Copyable alias on the flow node.**
The node's hover info currently uses a Radix `Tooltip`, which is not reliably clickable.
Replace the alias info Tooltip with an interactive Radix **HoverCard**
(`@radix-ui/react-hover-card` is already a core dependency) containing the request name, the
alias, and a **Copy** button. The button copies the raw alias via `useClipboardAdapter()`
(`writeText` returns a `Result`) and shows a transient "Copied!" state. This realizes the
user's "copy button on the node tooltip" choice with a primitive that actually supports
interaction.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

- **Backwards compatibility (intentional break):** Memory states backwards compatibility is
  not required. This feature deliberately changes (a) generated alias format and (b) the
  flow variable-reference grammar. Legacy references using the old dot form
  (`{{alias.$body.data.id}}`, no colon) will no longer resolve, and existing nodes keep their
  previously-stored aliases. See §7 for the user-facing impact note and the migration TODO.
- **New core dependency:** `jsonpath-plus` (^10.4.0, matching `packages/shared`). Allowed in
  `core` because it is pure JS; documented here per the dependency-discipline rule.

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no persistent schema changes. `FlowNode.alias` (string) already exists in
`packages/core/src/types/flow.ts`; only the *generated value format* changes. No new fields.

## 4. Files

**Create**
- `packages/core/src/utils/jsonPath.ts` — canonical JSONPath helper (`evaluateJsonPath`).
- `packages/core/src/components/ui/hover-card.tsx` — thin Radix HoverCard wrapper mirroring `ui/tooltip.tsx`.
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/jsonPath.test.ts` — helper unit tests.
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/flowResolver.test.ts` — resolver grammar + JSONPath tests.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/flow/FlowNode.test.tsx` — copy-alias UI test.

**Modify**
- `packages/core/package.json` — add `jsonpath-plus` dependency.
- `packages/core/src/utils/flowResolver.ts` — new grammar parser + JSONPath body resolution; remove `parseFlowPath`/`traverseJsonPath` and the body branch of `resolveBodyPath`; update JSDoc/examples.
- `packages/core/src/components/flow/requestNodeBuilder.ts` — readable-alias generation.
- `packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowNode.tsx` — HoverCard + copy button replacing the alias Tooltip.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/flow/requestNodeBuilder.test.ts` — update assertions to new alias format.
- `packages/mcp-server/docs/flow_feature.md` — add a "Variable references" section documenting the new syntax.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — note the new JSONPath support, readable aliases, copy affordance, and the breaking reference-syntax change.

## 5. Dependencies

- `jsonpath-plus` ^10.4.0 (new in `core`; already present in `shared`).
- `@radix-ui/react-hover-card` (already a `core` dependency).
- `useClipboardAdapter()` / `IClipboardAdapter` (already defined in `core`).

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Canonical JSONPath helper in `core`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add `jsonpath-plus` (^10.4.0) to `packages/core/package.json`
  dependencies and install. Create `packages/core/src/utils/jsonPath.ts` exporting
  `evaluateJsonPath(body: string, path: string): { value: unknown; values: unknown[]; found: boolean; error?: string }`.
  Implementation: `JSON.parse(body)` (catch → `{ found:false, error }`), then
  `JSONPath({ path, json, eval: false })`; `found = Array.isArray(results) && results.length > 0`;
  `value = found ? results[0] : undefined`; `values = found ? results : []`. JSDoc with the
  `eval:false` security rationale and an `@example`.
- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`jsonPath.test.ts`): scalar path (`$.data.id`); array index (`$.items[0].name`);
  recursive descent (`$..id`); wildcard (`$.users[*].name` → first value + full `values`);
  filter expression (`$.users[?(@.active)].id`); slice (`$.items[0:2]`); missing path → `found:false`;
  invalid JSON body → `found:false` with `error`; confirm `eval:false` (a filter that would
  require eval does not execute arbitrary code).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `evaluateJsonPath`; inline comment on the `eval:false` choice.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test jsonPath` passes; `jsonpath-plus` resolves in core build (`pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build`).

### TASK-002: Full JSONPath + new reference grammar in `flowResolver`

- [ ] **Implementation**: In `flowResolver.ts`, rewrite `parseVariableReference` to the new
  grammar (§2 Part B): return `{ alias: string | null; section: '$body'|'$headers'|'$status'|'$statusText'; subpath: string }`.
  Update `resolvePathInResponse` so the `$body` branch calls `evaluateJsonPath` from
  `utils/jsonPath` (prepend `$.` when `subpath` is non-empty and does not start with `$`;
  empty subpath → whole body) and stringifies the first match via `valueToString`; `$headers`
  uses the existing case-insensitive `resolveHeadersPath` with `subpath` as the header name;
  `$status`/`$statusText` unchanged. Remove `parseFlowPath`, `traverseJsonPath`, and the
  body-traversal logic in `resolveBodyPath` (drop the JSON content-type gate — attempt
  `JSON.parse` and let JSONPath decide; non-JSON with empty subpath still returns raw body).
  Update the file-header and `flowContextToDynamicEnvVars` JSDoc examples to the new syntax.
- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`flowResolver.test.ts`): build a `FlowContext` with sample responses and assert
  `flowContextToDynamicEnvVars` resolves: `{{alias.$body:$.data.id}}`; filter
  `{{alias.$body:$.users[?(@.active)].id}}`; recursive `{{alias.$body:$..id}}`; slice;
  `{{alias.$headers:content-type}}` (case-insensitive); `{{alias.$status}}` /
  `{{alias.$statusText}}`; no-alias `{{$body:$.data.id}}` resolving against the most-recent
  allowed alias; shorthand `{{$.data.id}}`; disallowed alias → not resolved; non-JSON body →
  whole-body for empty subpath, unresolved for a JSON path. Add a regression test asserting
  the legacy `{{alias.$body.data.id}}` dot-form no longer resolves (documents the break).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Rewrite the `flowResolver.ts` header comment and
  `flowContextToDynamicEnvVars` `@example` block to the new grammar.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test flowResolver` passes; manual flow run with a
  chained `{{alias.$body:$.data.id}}` reference resolves correctly.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: Readable alias generation in `requestNodeBuilder`

- [ ] **Implementation**: Update `buildAliasBase` in `requestNodeBuilder.ts`: lowercase →
  collapse non-alphanumeric runs to a single `-` → trim leading/trailing `-` → slice to
  `MAX_ALIAS_BASE_LENGTH` (20) → fall back to `alias` when no alphanumeric content remains.
  In `buildRequestNodes`, change the uniqueness suffix to `-N` starting at 2
  (`${aliasBase}-${suffix}`, suffix from 2), keeping the case-insensitive `usedAliases` set.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Update `requestNodeBuilder.test.ts` to the new format —
  "Get Users" → `get-users`; collisions → `get-users-2`, `get-users-3`; `!!!` →
  fallback `alias`; verify generated aliases contain no `$`, `.`, or `:` (guarantee relied on
  by the resolver). Update the existing `Get-Users1`/`request` assertions accordingly.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `buildAliasBase` describing the rules and the
  best-effort cross-flow consistency property; inline comment on the ordinal scheme.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test requestNodeBuilder` passes; adding two
  same-named requests in the canvas yields `get-users` / `get-users-2`.

### TASK-004: Copyable alias on the flow node

- [ ] **Implementation**: Create `packages/core/src/components/ui/hover-card.tsx` (thin Radix
  HoverCard wrapper mirroring `ui/tooltip.tsx`). In `FlowNode.tsx`, replace the alias info
  `Tooltip` (the one showing name + "Alias:") with a `HoverCard` whose content shows the
  request name, the alias (monospace), and a Copy button (lucide `Copy` → `Check` on success).
  Wire the button to `useClipboardAdapter().writeText(node.alias)`, handle the `Result`
  (transient "Copied!" on `isOk`; on error, `notification.showNotification('error', …)` per
  memory's error-handling rule), and reset after ~1.5s. Keep the Input/Output handle Tooltips
  unchanged. Preserve the truncated alias/name display on the node card itself.
- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`FlowNode.test.tsx`): render `FlowNode` inside `<AdapterProvider>` with a
  `createMockAdapter()` whose clipboard is spied; open the hover card, click Copy, assert
  `clipboard.writeText` called with the alias and that the "Copied!" state appears; assert the
  alias text is rendered.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new `hover-card.tsx` wrapper and on the FlowNode
  copy handler; brief inline note on why HoverCard replaces Tooltip (interactivity).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test FlowNode` passes; hovering a node in the
  canvas reveals the card and Copy places the alias on the clipboard.

### TASK-005: User-facing docs & changelog

- [ ] **Implementation**: Add a "Variable references" section to
  `packages/mcp-server/docs/flow_feature.md` documenting the new grammar, the four sections,
  JSONPath support (with filter/recursive/slice examples), the no-alias/shorthand forms, and
  how to copy an alias from a node. Update `CHANGELOG.md` with: full JSONPath support in flow
  references, readable aliases, node copy-alias affordance, and a **Breaking** bullet for the
  changed reference syntax + alias format.

- **Verify**: Docs render correctly; CHANGELOG entry present under the next version heading.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002, TASK-003, TASK-004

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — no auth surface touched.
- **Error Handling**:
  - JSONPath/JSON-parse failures are contained in `evaluateJsonPath` (returns `found:false` +
    `error`); the resolver treats unresolved references as "not provided" (existing behavior),
    so a malformed reference simply leaves the `{{…}}` placeholder unresolved rather than throwing.
  - Clipboard copy uses the `Result` pattern; failures surface via
    `notification.showNotification('error', …)` (never `alert`).
  - `eval:false` on `JSONPath` prevents script execution through filter expressions.
- **Logging**: N/A — no new server/dev-log surface. (No secrets are ever logged.)
- **Breaking-change impact (user-facing)**: Existing flows that reference responses with the
  old dot syntax (`{{alias.$body.data.id}}`) or that relied on the old alias format will not
  resolve until updated to the new colon/`$`-rooted form. This is called out in the CHANGELOG
  "Breaking" note. An optional one-time auto-migration of stored references is tracked in
  `.spec-lite/TODO.md` (out of scope for this feature).
